title: rumex aquaticus subsp. protractus
intro: Rumex aquaticus subsp. protractus, also known as common waterdock or protracted dock, is a subspecies of the Rumex aquaticus plant, a perennial aquatic plant that grows in wetlands and riverbanks. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Rumex aquaticus subsp. protractus include its ability to reduce fever, treat wounds, and alleviate digestive issues. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion for use in herbal remedies, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
